data.title: LSTrans: Efficient Knowledge Transfer for Lightweight and Automated ECG Classification
data.description: arXiv:2607.10784v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Deploying deep learning models for automated electrocardiogram classification on resource-constrained wearable devices remains challenging due to high computational costs. To address this, we propose LSTrans, a lightweight hybrid model designed for efficient and sensitive ECG analysis. LSTrans introduces a specialized 1D convolutional backbone with an interleaved layer architecture to capture both macroscopic rhythmic trends and microscopic morphological variations. This backbone is cascaded with a Transformer encoder to model long-range temporal dependencies, incorporating Low-Rank Adaptation across critical layers to compress the model and reduce the trainable parameter space. We further employ homogeneous and heterogeneous knowledge distillation to transfer diagnostic expertise from high-capacity teacher models to the student. Experimental results on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that LSTrans achieves a competitive balance between diagnostic sensitivity and resource efficiency, substantially reducing peak memory footprints and training latency during downstream adaptation. The source code is available for review at https://github.com/zyee00128/LSTrans4BIBM.
data.author: Yi Zhao, Jiajun Gao, Chenyang Xu, Yuxi Zhou, Hao Wang
